ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater (Mango Sandstorm / TA450) RMM-based initial access — legitimate remote-access tools spawned by Outlook or browser
ucs.description: Hunts the TA450 / Mango Sandstorm hallmark of phishing recipients into installing legitimate Remote Monitoring & Management (RMM) tooling — ScreenConnect/ConnectWise Control, Atera, RemoteUtilities, SimpleHelp, AnyDesk, Syncro — by detecting the RMM client binary executing as a child of a delivery vector (Outlook, browser, Explorer, or a freshly-spawned msiexec). MuddyWater abandoned bespoke implants for off-the-shelf RMM in 2022-2024 campaigns precisely because the binary is signed and bypasses AV; the tell is the parent process and that the host has never run that RMM tool before.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","explorer.exe","msiexec.exe","7zg.exe","winrar.exe","7zfm.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ConnectWiseControl.Client.exe","atera.exe","AteraAgent.exe","AgentPackageSTA.exe","rutserv.exe","rutview.exe","AnyDesk.exe","SimpleHelp.exe","RemoteUtilities.exe","RemoteUtilitiesViewer.exe","syncro.exe","WinAgent.exe","NinjaRMMAgent.exe","Action1_agent.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where user!="*$" | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | rename host as dest
ucs.kql:
let RMMBinaries = dynamic(["screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","screenconnect.clientservice.exe","connectwisecontrol.client.exe","atera.exe","ateraagent.exe","agentpackagesta.exe","rutserv.exe","rutview.exe","anydesk.exe","simplehelp.exe","remoteutilities.exe","remoteutilitiesviewer.exe","syncro.exe","winagent.exe","ninjarmmagent.exe","action1_agent.exe"]);
let DeliveryParents = dynamic(["outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","explorer.exe","msiexec.exe","7zg.exe","winrar.exe","7zfm.exe"]);
let RmmBaseline = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp between (ago(45d) .. ago(2h))
    | where FileName in~ (RMMBinaries)
    | summarize by DeviceId, FileName;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ (RMMBinaries)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (DeliveryParents)
   or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\")
| join kind=leftanti RmmBaseline on DeviceId, FileName
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          Child = FileName,
          ChildPath = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256, IsRemote = IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let RMMBinaries = dynamic(["screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","screenconnect.clientservice.exe","connectwisecontrol.client.exe","atera.exe","ateraagent.exe","agentpackagesta.exe","rutserv.exe","rutview.exe","anydesk.exe","simplehelp.exe","remoteutilities.exe","remoteutilitiesviewer.exe","syncro.exe","winagent.exe","ninjarmmagent.exe","action1_agent.exe"]);
let DeliveryParents = dynamic(["outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","explorer.exe","msiexec.exe","7zg.exe","winrar.exe","7zfm.exe"]);
let RmmBaseline = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(45d) .. ago(2h))
    | where EventID == 4688
    | extend ChildName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ChildName in (RMMBinaries)
    | summarize by Computer, ChildName;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User" and Account !endswith "$"
| extend ChildName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         ParentName = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildName in (RMMBinaries)
| where ParentName in (DeliveryParents)
   or NewProcessName has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\")
| join kind=leftanti RmmBaseline on $left.Computer == $right.Computer, $left.ChildName == $right.ChildName
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, ParentName,
          NewProcessName, ChildName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: MuddyWater Mango Sandstorm RMM Tool Spawned by Office Outlook or Browser
id: 7d9b1c20-9f44-4f0d-9c6c-mw01rmm0001
status: experimental
description: Detects MuddyWater (Mango Sandstorm / TA450 / Static Kitten / Seedworm) initial-access tradecraft where a phishing payload deploys legitimate RMM software (ScreenConnect, Atera, AnyDesk, RemoteUtilities, SimpleHelp, Syncro, etc.) by capturing those signed RMM clients executing as children of mail/browser/archive delivery processes.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0069/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/04/18/mercury-and-deev-team-cooperate-to-conduct-destructive-attacks/
  - https://www.proofpoint.com/us/blog/threat-insight/ta450-uses-embedded-links-pdf-attachments-latest-campaign
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa22-055a
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026-05-10
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  parent_delivery:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\brave.exe'
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\7zg.exe'
      - '\winrar.exe'
      - '\7zfm.exe'
  rmm_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe'
      - '\ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe'
      - '\ConnectWiseControl.Client.exe'
      - '\atera.exe'
      - '\AteraAgent.exe'
      - '\AgentPackageSTA.exe'
      - '\rutserv.exe'
      - '\rutview.exe'
      - '\AnyDesk.exe'
      - '\SimpleHelp.exe'
      - '\RemoteUtilities.exe'
      - '\RemoteUtilitiesViewer.exe'
      - '\syncro.exe'
      - '\WinAgent.exe'
      - '\NinjaRMMAgent.exe'
      - '\Action1_agent.exe'
  condition: parent_delivery and rmm_child
falsepositives:
  - Sanctioned IT helpdesk RMM rollouts via msiexec from approved package paths — allow-list by host group / install path.
  - End-user reinstall of an authorised RMM agent from the vendor portal opened in a browser.
level: high
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.t1219.002
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1566
  - attack.t1204.001
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.g0069
ucs.rationale: MuddyWater / Mango Sandstorm / TA450 has been Iran's most prolific RMM-abuse cluster since 2022; Microsoft, Proofpoint, Symantec (Seedworm) and CISA AA22-055A all document the exact tooling list above as their persistent C2 surface. Generic RMM detection alone is noisy, but combining (a) signed RMM client as the child binary, (b) a delivery-vector parent (mail/browser/archive/freshly-spawned msiex

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater POWERSTATS / PowGoop loader — Office macro spawning PowerShell or LOLBin with encoded/obfuscated command line
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical MuddyWater (Seedworm / TEMP.Zagros) Office-macro-to-PowerShell loader chain that has shipped POWERSTATS, PowGoop, MuddyC3 / PhonyC2, and Small Sieve since 2017. Detects when winword/excel/powerpnt/outlook spawns powershell, mshta, rundll32, cscript, wscript, or cmd with the obfuscation primitives MuddyWater is documented using (-EncodedCommand, FromBase64String, IEX/Invoke-Expression, hidden window, -nop, certutil/bitsadmin staging).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","regsvr32.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where user!="*$" | where match(process, "(?i)(-e(nc|ncodedcommand|c)\s+[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{40,}|FromBase64String|DownloadString|Invoke-Expression|IEX\s*\(|-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+hidden|-w\s+hidden|-nop\s|bitsadmin\s+/transfer|certutil\s+-(urlcache|decode|encode|f)|new-object\s+net\.webclient|hxxp|wscript\.shell)") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | rename host as dest
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","regsvr32.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-EncodedCommand"," -enc "," -EC ","FromBase64String","DownloadString","Invoke-Expression","IEX(","-w hidden","-WindowStyle Hidden","-nop ","bitsadmin /transfer","certutil -decode","certutil -urlcache","New-Object Net.WebClient","WScript.Shell")
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-e[nc]+\s+[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{40,}"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          Child = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256,
          Integrity = ProcessIntegrityLevel,
          IsRemote = IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User" and Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentName = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         ChildName  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,  "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentName in ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe")
| where ChildName  in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","regsvr32.exe")
| where CommandLine has_any ("-EncodedCommand"," -enc "," -EC ","FromBase64String","DownloadString","Invoke-Expression","IEX(","-w hidden","-WindowStyle Hidden","-nop ","bitsadmin /transfer","certutil -decode","certutil -urlcache","New-Object Net.WebClient","WScript.Shell")
   or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-e[nc]+\s+[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{40,}"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: MuddyWater POWERSTATS PowGoop Office Macro Loader Chain
id: 4f1a93c8-5e2d-4ca6-a1b7-mw02office01
status: experimental
description: Detects MuddyWater (Seedworm / Mango Sandstorm / TEMP.Zagros / Static Kitten / TA450) macro-to-loader execution where Office or Equation Editor spawns PowerShell, mshta, rundll32, cscript, wscript, regsvr32 or cmd carrying obfuscation primitives associated with POWERSTATS, PowGoop, MuddyC3, PhonyC2 and Small Sieve loaders.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0069/
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa22-055a
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2022/iranian-apt-muddywater-targets-turkish-users-via-malicious-pdfs-executables/
  - https://www.deepinstinct.com/blog/powgoop-malware-variants-revealing-recent-muddywater-activity
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026-05-10
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  parent_office:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\eqnedt32.exe'
  child_interpreter:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
  obfuscation_primitive:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '-EncodedCommand'
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -EC '
      - 'FromBase64String'
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'Invoke-Expression'
      - 'IEX('
      - '-w hidden'
      - '-WindowStyle Hidden'
      - '-nop '
      - 'bitsadmin /transfer'
      - 'certutil -decode'
      - 'certutil -urlcache'
      - 'New-Object Net.WebClient'
      - 'WScript.Shell'
  condition: parent_office and child_interpreter and obfuscation_primitive
falsepositives:
  - Internal automation that legitimately invokes PowerShell from Office (rare; document parent macro signer and allow-list).
level: high
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1027
  - attack.t1027.010
  - attack.t1137.001
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.t1218.011
  - attack.t1140
  - attack.g0069
ucs.rationale: Office-spawned PowerShell with obfuscation has been MuddyWater's signature execution primitive across every major report from 2017 through 2024 — POWERSTATS (Symantec/Seedworm), PowGoop and Mori (Deep Instinct), MuddyC3, PhonyC2 (Group-IB), Small Sieve (NCSC) all chain MS-Office macro → encoded/obfuscated PowerShell or LOLBin. CISA AA22-055A and Microsoft's Mango Sandstorm advisories specifically

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
